user.title: AI Camera Centre
user.description: Set up the global defaults. You can change all of these later, and add cameras, alert targets and known people from the integration page once setup finishes.

capture.name: Capture & analysis
capture.description: What each trigger captures, and how often a camera may re-run.

data_description.snapshot_count: How many frames are grabbed for each event. More frames give the AI more to compare, but take longer to capture.
data_description.snapshot_interval_ms: Milliseconds between frames in the burst.
data_description.cooldown_seconds: Per camera. Motion within this many seconds of the last analysis is ignored, preventing alert storms.
data_description.ai_task_entity: Which AI provider analyses the snapshots. Leave blank to use Home Assistant's default AI Task entity.

alerts.name: Alerts & history
alerts.description: Which alerts are kept, for how long, and where notifications open.

data_description.min_log_score: Only archive alerts at or above this suspicion score (1 logs every real alert). Filtered-out alerts can still notify.
data_description.retention_days: Alerts and their images older than this are pruned automatically.
data_description.repeat_context_minutes: Feeds a camera's recent alerts back to the AI so it can spot the same person returning or loitering. 0 disables it.
data_description.log_window_start: Leave both times blank to log at any hour.
data_description.log_window_end: Alerts outside the window are not archived, but can still notify.
data_description.dashboard_path: The path a tapped notification opens, e.g. /lovelace/alerts.

alarm.name: Alarm & Alarmo
alarm.description: Optional alarm panel. Required for the armed-based notify and processing rules, and for triggering Alarmo.

data_description.alarm_panel_entity: Your alarm_control_panel entity. Enables the 'armed' notify conditions and Alarmo triggering.
data_description.alarmo_enabled: Only fires while the panel is already armed.
data_description.alarmo_trigger_score: Score at or above which Alarmo is triggered.

processing.name: Motion processing (house default)
processing.description: Skip AI analysis entirely unless presence, alarm state and time all allow it — saving cost and noise. Each camera can follow this or set its own. 'Analyze now' and the service always bypass it.

data_description.process_presence: e.g. only when nobody is home.
data_description.process_armed: Needs an alarm panel set above.
data_description.process_time_mode: Any time, a fixed window, or daytime/nighttime from your sun entity.
data_description.process_time_start: Used when 'Between two times' is selected.
data_description.process_time_end: Used when 'Between two times' is selected.
data_description.sun_entity: Drives the daytime/nighttime option. Default sun.sun.

ai.name: AI response style
ai.description: Optional personality for alert wording. Never changes the score or the factual fields.

data_description.response_style: e.g. "in the style of Samuel L. Jackson".

abort.single_instance_allowed: AI Camera Centre is already configured.

init.title: AI Camera Centre settings
init.description: Global defaults for every camera. Add or edit cameras, alert targets and known people from the integration page.

user.title: Add camera
user.description: Each camera becomes its own device with its own entities. Any of its motion triggers turning on starts the AI analysis.

data_description.name: A friendly label, e.g. "Side Gate".
data_description.camera_entity: The camera.* entity that snapshots are taken from.
data_description.motion_entities: Any of these turning on starts the analysis. Leave blank to trigger only via the Analyze button or the ai_camera_centre.analyze service. If a camera stops triggering after an update, re-select its trigger here — entity ids can change when the source integration changes.
data_description.scene_context: Describe what the camera sees and say explicitly whether a gate is visible. Added to the AI prompt.

processing.name: Motion processing
processing.description: By default this camera follows the house-wide motion-processing rules. Choose 'Custom for this camera' to override them here.

data_description.motion_policy: Follow the house settings, or set custom rules below.
data_description.process_presence: Only used when the policy is 'Custom for this camera'.
data_description.process_armed: Only used when the policy is 'Custom for this camera'.
data_description.process_time_mode: Only used when the policy is 'Custom for this camera'.
data_description.process_time_start: Used when 'Between two times' is selected.
data_description.process_time_end: Used when 'Between two times' is selected.

reconfigure.title: Edit camera
reconfigure.description: Renaming keeps the camera's existing alert history. Any of its motion triggers turning on starts the AI analysis.

data_description.name: A friendly label, e.g. "Side Gate".
data_description.camera_entity: The camera.* entity that snapshots are taken from.
data_description.motion_entities: Any of these turning on starts the analysis. Leave blank to trigger only via the Analyze button or the ai_camera_centre.analyze service. If a camera stops triggering after an update, re-select its trigger here — entity ids can change when the source integration changes.
data_description.scene_context: Describe what the camera sees and say explicitly whether a gate is visible. Added to the AI prompt.

processing.name: Motion processing
processing.description: By default this camera follows the house-wide motion-processing rules. Choose 'Custom for this camera' to override them here.

data_description.motion_policy: Follow the house settings, or set custom rules below.
data_description.process_presence: Only used when the policy is 'Custom for this camera'.
data_description.process_armed: Only used when the policy is 'Custom for this camera'.
data_description.process_time_mode: Only used when the policy is 'Custom for this camera'.
data_description.process_time_start: Used when 'Between two times' is selected.
data_description.process_time_end: Used when 'Between two times' is selected.

error.invalid_name: Please enter a valid camera name.
error.duplicate_camera: A camera with this name already exists.

user.title: Add alert target
user.description: An alert target is a notify service that receives camera alerts. Set the minimum suspicion score it should be woken for, when it should be notified, and optionally which cameras it covers.

data_description.name: A friendly label, e.g. "Ben's phone". Derived from the service if left blank.
data_description.service: The notify.* service that receives the alert.
data_description.min_score: 1 notifies on every alert; 6+ only for genuinely suspicious events.
data_description.notify_condition: Always, or only when nobody's home / the alarm is armed / either. The armed options need an alarm panel set in Settings.
data_description.cameras: Leave blank to cover all cameras.

reconfigure.title: Edit alert target
reconfigure.description: Set the minimum suspicion score this target should be woken for, when it should be notified, and optionally which cameras it covers.

data_description.name: A friendly label, e.g. "Ben's phone". Derived from the service if left blank.
data_description.service: The notify.* service that receives the alert.
data_description.min_score: 1 notifies on every alert; 6+ only for genuinely suspicious events.
data_description.notify_condition: Always, or only when nobody's home / the alarm is armed / either. The armed options need an alarm panel set in Settings.
data_description.cameras: Leave blank to cover all cameras.

error.invalid_service: Please select or enter a notify service.

user.title: Add known visitor
user.description: Describe a household member or regular so the AI recognises them and treats them as low-risk. The description is added to every camera's prompt.

data_description.name: e.g. Ben, or "the postman".
data_description.description: Include distinguishing features — build, typical clothing, whether they use a wheelchair, a pet, etc.

reconfigure.title: Edit known visitor
reconfigure.description: The description is added to every camera's prompt.

data_description.name: e.g. Ben, or "the postman".
data_description.description: Include distinguishing features — build, typical clothing, a pet, etc.

error.duplicate_visitor: A known person with this name already exists.
